back_translation: At that time, King Herod began arresting some members of the church in order to persecute them.
Herod ordered the execution of James, the older brother of John, with the sword.
When he saw that this pleased the Jews, Herod also went on to arrest Peter. At that time it was during the Feast of Unleavened Bread.

When he had arrested Peter, Herod put him in prison and handed him over to four squads of soldiers, four men per squad, to keep watch — intending that when the Passover festival had passed he would bring him out before the people.
So Peter was kept imprisoned in the jail, but the church prayed to God for him earnestly.
On the night before Herod was going to bring him out for trial, Peter was sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains, and sentries were keeping watch at the door of the prison.
Suddenly, an angel of the Lord appeared, and a light shone in the cell. The angel tapped Peter's side, woke him up, and said, 'Get up quickly.' Then the chains fell off from his hands.
The angel told him, 'Gird your waist-belt and put on your sandals.' Peter did so. The angel also said, 'Put on your cloak and follow me.'
Peter followed the angel out, but he did not know that what the angel was doing was real. He thought he was seeing a vision.

When they had passed the first and the second guard-posts, they came to the iron gate that opened out into the city, and that gate opened by itself for them. When they had gone out and walked the length of one street, the angel left Peter immediately.
When Peter came to his senses, he said, 'Now I know for certain that the Lord has sent his angel to rescue me from the grip of Herod, and from everything that the Jews were expecting to happen.'
When he understood this matter, he went to the house of Mary, the mother of John who was also called Mark, where many people had gathered together and were praying.
When Peter knocked at the courtyard-gate, a servant girl named Rhoda came to the gate.
When she recognized Peter's voice, she was so overjoyed that she did not open the gate, but instead ran inside and told everyone that Peter was standing in front of the gate.
But they said to her, 'You've gone crazy.' She, however, kept insisting that it was really so. Then they said, 'It must be his angel.'
But Peter kept on knocking at the door without stopping. When they opened the door and saw him, they were completely astonished.

Peter motioned with his hand for them to be silent, then told them how the Lord had brought him out of the prison. He said, 'Go and tell James and all the brothers about these things.' Then he went out to another place.
At daybreak, the soldiers were in great commotion about where Peter had gone.
When Herod had searched for Peter and did not find him, he examined the guards and ordered them to be executed. Then Herod went down from the region of Judea to stay at Caesarea for a time.

At that time Herod was very angry and in severe conflict with the people of Tyre and Sidon. They therefore came to him in agreement. When they had persuaded Blastus, the keeper of the king's bedchamber, to be on their side, they then asked for peace, because their region depended on food supplies from the king's country.
On the appointed day, Herod put on royal clothing, sat on the throne, and delivered a public address to them.
The crowd shouted, 'This is the voice of a god, not of a man!'
Immediately, an angel of the Lord struck Herod down, because Herod had not given glory to God. Then he was eaten by worms and died.
But the word of God continued to grow and multiply.
As for Barnabas and Saul, when they had completed their ministry of relief in Jerusalem, they traveled back, taking John, who was also called Mark, with them.
